name: Pillow
summary: Friendly Python Imaging Library fork for opening, manipulating, and saving image files.

story.problem: You need to resize user avatars, add watermarks, convert uploaded PNGs to WebP, extract EXIF data, or generate thumbnails — all server-side without a heavy graphics toolkit.
story.mental_model: Pillow represents any image as an Image object with a mode (RGB, RGBA, L), size (width, height), and pixel data. Operations return new Image objects (immutable pipeline). When done, call .save() or .tobytes().
story.when_to_use: Resizing/cropping/converting images, compositing overlays, generating thumbnails, basic drawing, reading EXIF metadata, converting between formats.
story.when_not_to_use: High-performance batch processing at scale (use ImageMagick or libvips/pyvips). Video processing. Complex image recognition (use OpenCV or ML models).

alternatives.name: pyvips
alternatives.reason: 10-100x faster for large images, low memory. Steeper API but worth it for scale.

alternatives.name: opencv-python
alternatives.reason: Computer vision operations, real-time video, more ML-friendly.

alternatives.name: wand
alternatives.reason: Python bindings for ImageMagick — broader format support, more effects.

nodes.label: PIL (Pillow)
nodes.summary: Import from PIL: `from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFilter`.

nodes.label: PIL.Image
nodes.summary: Core module. Image.open(), Image.new(), Image.fromarray(). Returns Image objects.

nodes.label: PIL.ImageDraw
nodes.summary: Draw lines, rectangles, ellipses, text on an image. `draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)`.

nodes.label: PIL.ImageFilter
nodes.summary: Filters: BLUR, SHARPEN, EDGE_ENHANCE, GaussianBlur(radius). Apply with img.filter(...).

nodes.label: PIL.ImageFont
nodes.summary: Load fonts for text rendering: ImageFont.truetype('font.ttf', 24).

nodes.label: PIL.ImageOps
nodes.summary: High-level operations: fit(), contain(), grayscale(), invert(), autocontrast().

nodes.label: PIL.ExifTags
nodes.summary: Read EXIF metadata: `img._getexif()` returns raw EXIF dict; use ExifTags.TAGS to decode keys.

steps.label: Open
steps.code:
from PIL import Image
img = Image.open('photo.jpg')

steps.label: Thumbnail
steps.code:
img.thumbnail((400, 400))  # preserves aspect ratio
img.save('thumb.jpg', quality=85, optimize=True)

steps.label: Setup
steps.code:
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
img = Image.open('photo.jpg').convert('RGBA')

steps.label: Overlay
steps.code:
overlay = Image.new('RGBA', img.size, (0,0,0,0))
draw = ImageDraw.Draw(overlay)
font = ImageFont.truetype('arial.ttf', 36)
draw.text((20, 20), '© MyBrand', fill=(255,255,255,128), font=font)

steps.label: Batch
steps.code:
from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image

for p in Path('images').glob('*.png'):
    img = Image.open(p)
    img.save(p.with_suffix('.webp'), 'WEBP', quality=80)
